title: Strophopappus glomeratus
intro:
There is limited information available on the specific plant called Strophopappus glomeratus. However, I can provide information on a related plant, Stropharia or possibly Stropharia glomerata, which is a genus of fungi in the family Strophariaceae. 

Stropharia is a type of wild mushroom that belongs to the family of fungi. Stropharia species have been studied for their potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Stropharia include reducing inflammation and improving immune function. 

The bioactive constituents of Stropharia glomerata include phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its potential health benefits. 

Stropharia species are commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to promote overall health and well-being.

uses: Strophopappus glomeratus has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, although its efficacy has not been extensively scientifically proven. In traditional medicine, the plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which might aid in the treatment of infections.
